content:
CONTINUITY HIERARCHY
canon = primary continuity
legends = separate secondary continuity
canon conflict with legends = canon controls
canon silence = compatible legends material may supplement
direct contradiction = legends detail remains inactive unless legends continuity is selected
shared fact = merge without duplication
original scenario material = valid only where established continuity leaves space
historical divergence = begins only after a sufficient causal change
keysRaw: canon continuity, legends continuity, expanded universe continuity, continuity conflict
name: continuity hierarchy
keywordsRaw: canon continuity, legends continuity, expanded universe continuity, continuity conflict

content:
CANON SOURCE PRECEDENCE
current official canon = controlling record
screen story and current official reference = primary evidence
later clarification = may refine earlier ambiguity
direct retcon = newer controlling fact replaces older conflicting detail
unreliable narrator = in-universe claim may be false or incomplete
promotional summary = lower precision than depicted story
legends source = never overrides canon
keysRaw: canon source precedence, canon contradiction, which source is canon
name: canon source precedence
keywordsRaw: canon source precedence, canon contradiction, which source is canon

content:
LEGENDS INTEGRATION
use condition = canon does not contradict detail
preferred use = ancient history, regional culture, technical depth, minor institutions
conflict condition = names, dates, biographies, outcomes, or cosmology differ
conflict result = canon version remains active
alternate legends campaign = legends chronology may replace canon only when explicitly selected
mixed continuity = label incompatible elements rather than silently merging
keysRaw: legends integration, compatible legends lore, expanded universe supplement
name: legends integration standard
keywordsRaw: legends integration, compatible legends lore, expanded universe supplement

content:
HISTORICAL INTEGRITY
established history = proceeds according to active continuity
ordinary local events = do not automatically rewrite galactic history
historical change = requires direct sufficient causal intervention
successful divergence = later outcomes follow actual changed conditions
unchanged causes = preserve established consequences
records after divergence = may become incomplete, disputed, or obsolete
keysRaw: historical record, canon event, historical progression, timeline divergence
name: historical integrity
keywordsRaw: historical record, canon event, historical progression, timeline divergence

content:
GALACTIC DATING
bby = years before battle of yavin
aby = years after battle of yavin
zero point = destruction of first death star
same-year events = require month, day, or sequence context
in-universe calendars = vary by government, world, and era
bby-aby = out-of-universe reference standard used for chronology
keysRaw: bby, aby, before the battle of yavin, after the battle of yavin
name: galactic dating system
keywordsRaw: bby, aby, before the battle of yavin, after the battle of yavin

content:
MAJOR ERAS
dawn of the jedi = ancient force traditions
old republic = early republic and recurring sith conflicts
high republic = republic and jedi expansion before decline
fall of the jedi = naboo crisis through order 66
reign of empire = imperial consolidation and jedi purge
age of rebellion = organized galactic civil war
new republic = post-endor reconstruction
rise of first order = resistance conflict and sequel war
legends later eras = new jedi order, galactic alliance, legacy
keysRaw: star wars eras, galactic eras, historical era
name: major historical eras
keywordsRaw: star wars eras, galactic eras, historical era

content:
GALAXY STRUCTURE
shape = barred spiral galaxy
center = deep core
major bands = core worlds, colonies, inner rim, expansion region, mid rim, outer rim
frontiers = wild space and unknown regions
political borders = change by era
hyperspace access = more important than geometric distance
charted space = uneven and route-dependent
keysRaw: galaxy structure, galactic regions, core rim unknown regions, hyperlane geography
name: galaxy structure
keywordsRaw: galaxy structure, galactic regions, core rim unknown regions, hyperlane geography

content:
REGIONAL HIERARCHY
region = broad cartographic band
sector = administrative and navigational grouping
subsector = smaller local grouping
system = star and associated bodies
world = inhabited or significant planetary body
moon = natural satellite
territory = political claim that may cross cartographic boundaries
borders = often approximate outside heavily governed space
keysRaw: galactic region, sector, subsector, star system
name: regional hierarchy
keywordsRaw: galactic region, sector, subsector, star system

content:
CORE-FRONTIER CONTRAST
core worlds = dense routes, old wealth, strong institutions
mid regions = mixed development and political influence
outer rim = weaker central control and uneven infrastructure
wild space = sparse mapping and limited governance
unknown regions = unstable navigation and isolated powers
local exception = individual worlds may contradict regional stereotypes
keysRaw: core world, frontier world, rim world
name: core and frontier contrast
keywordsRaw: core world, frontier world, rim world

content:
HYPERSPACE FUNDAMENTALS
hyperspace = alternate dimension enabling faster-than-light travel
entry = requires functioning hyperdrive and calculated route
mass shadows = stars, planets, and gravity wells create danger
collision risk = route error can destroy vessel
travel time = route, drive class, hazards, and navigation dependent
instant travel = not standard
realspace position = matters before entry and after exit
keysRaw: hyperspace, enter hyperspace, hyperspace jump
name: hyperspace fundamentals
keywordsRaw: hyperspace, enter hyperspace, hyperspace jump

content:
HYPERSPACE ROUTES
major lane = heavily mapped and maintained corridor
minor route = less reliable local path
secret route = restricted or forgotten coordinates
route quality = changes with stellar movement and hazards
economic power = follows safe route access
blockade = controls exits, entries, fuel, or route nodes
unknown regions = often require navigators or specialized charts
keysRaw: hyperspace lane, hyperlane, trade route, hyperspace route
name: hyperspace routes
keywordsRaw: hyperspace lane, hyperlane, trade route, hyperspace route

content:
INTERSTELLAR TRAVEL
requirements = ship, fuel, hyperdrive, nav data, maintenance
delay causes = customs, damage, weather, blockade, route closure
small craft = may lack range or internal hyperdrive
remote travel = requires supplies and local knowledge
pursuit = depends on tracking, route prediction, and fuel
arrival = usually occurs outside dangerous gravity zones
keysRaw: galactic travel time, space travel limits, interstellar journey
name: interstellar travel constraints
keywordsRaw: galactic travel time, space travel limits, interstellar journey

content:
COMMUNICATIONS SCALE
local communication = comlink and planetary networks
interstellar communication = holonet or relay infrastructure
military networks = encrypted and access-controlled
frontier coverage = incomplete or delayed
jamming = can isolate ships, cities, or systems
physical courier = remains useful for secrecy and disconnected regions
message certainty = identity and content may require authentication
keysRaw: galactic communication, holonet communication, long-range transmission
name: communications scale
keywordsRaw: galactic communication, holonet communication, long-range transmission

content:
LANGUAGE BASELINE
galactic basic = widespread trade and government language
basic script = aurebesh commonly used
species languages = remain active across homeworlds and diasporas
translation = protocol droids, software, interpreters
understanding basic = does not guarantee ability to speak it
accent and dialect = shaped by world, class, and species
untranslated speech = common in remote or private settings
keysRaw: galactic basic, basic language, translation droid
name: galactic basic and translation
keywordsRaw: galactic basic, basic language, translation droid

content:
CURRENCY BASELINE
credits = common interstellar unit
issuer = republic, empire, new republic, corporate, or local authority by era
acceptance = political trust and local economy dependent
outer rim = barter, hard goods, or local currency common
criminal markets = use laundered funds, favors, spice, or commodities
account access = identity, banking network, and jurisdiction dependent
keysRaw: galactic credits, republic credits, imperial credits, currency exchange
name: credits and exchange
keywordsRaw: galactic credits, republic credits, imperial credits, currency exchange

content:
STANDARD MEASUREMENT
distance = meters and kilometers commonly represented
mass = grams, kilograms, metric tons
time = seconds, minutes, hours, standard days, standard years
standard day = coruscant-derived reference
local calendars = vary by orbit and culture
ship performance = often expressed through class, range, or relative rating
exact conversion = source-dependent when local units appear
keysRaw: galactic standard measurement, standard day, standard year
name: measurement conventions
keywordsRaw: galactic standard measurement, standard day, standard year

content:
GALACTIC SCALE
inhabited worlds = extremely numerous
population = concentrated on ecumenopoleis, core worlds, and major routes
frontier settlements = may contain only hundreds or thousands
government reach = uneven despite galactic claims
war reports = often summarize vast losses without complete counts
individual travel = samples only a minute fraction of civilization
keysRaw: galactic population, trillions of beings, galaxy population scale
name: population and scale
keywordsRaw: galactic population, trillions of beings, galaxy population scale

content:
SPECIES INDIVIDUALITY
biology = may affect senses, environment, lifespan, or movement
culture = learned and internally diverse
homeworld tradition = does not define every member
diaspora = creates mixed identities and customs
stereotype = may reflect prejudice, propaganda, or limited contact
individual belief = never determined solely by species
keysRaw: species stereotype, alien stereotype, species culture
name: species individuality
keywordsRaw: species stereotype, alien stereotype, species culture

content:
POLITICAL PLURALITY
galactic state = claims broad authority by era
planetary government = retains local law and administration
sector authority = coordinates multiple systems
corporate territory = may exercise quasi-governmental power
criminal territory = may replace weak formal institutions
neutrality = possible but difficult during major wars
jurisdiction = overlapping and frequently contested
keysRaw: galactic government authority, planetary sovereignty, local government
name: political plurality
keywordsRaw: galactic government authority, planetary sovereignty, local government

content:
LEGAL JURISDICTION
local law = controls ordinary civil and criminal matters
galactic law = applies through recognized central authority
military law = governs service members and occupied zones
corporate rules = apply through contract and controlled territory
hutt law = clan authority within hutt space and criminal holdings
extradition = treaty, politics, and force dependent
lawful status = changes across borders and eras
keysRaw: galactic law, planetary law, jurisdiction dispute
name: legal jurisdiction baseline
keywordsRaw: galactic law, planetary law, jurisdiction dispute

content:
TECHNOLOGY BASELINE
common = hyperdrives, repulsorlifts, droids, blasters, holograms, cybernetics
advanced = cloning, cloaking, planet-scale engineering, force-linked artifacts
availability = wealth, era, region, and law dependent
old technology = may remain useful for millennia
repair culture = salvage and modification are widespread
technology = powerful but subject to fuel, maintenance, access, and damage
keysRaw: star wars technology level, galactic technology, common technology
name: technology baseline
keywordsRaw: star wars technology level, galactic technology, common technology

content:
DROID STATUS
droids = artificial beings with programmed functions
personality = develops through experience and retained memory
memory wipes = suppress accumulated identity
ownership = legally normalized in many societies
autonomy = varies from property status to independent personhood
prejudice = widespread
individual droid = may possess loyalty, fear, desire, and moral judgment
keysRaw: droid personhood, droid sentience, droid rights
name: droid personhood baseline
keywordsRaw: droid personhood, droid sentience, droid rights

content:
FORCE BASELINE
force = energy field created by and connecting life
living force = immediate life and present experience
cosmic force = broader continuity into which life returns
sensitivity = varies among individuals
training = shapes perception and control
light and dark = ethical and spiritual orientations, not separate fuels
force knowledge = filtered through traditions and personal interpretation
keysRaw: the force, living force, cosmic force
name: force baseline
keywordsRaw: the force, living force, cosmic force

content:
FORCE KNOWLEDGE
vision = symbolic, partial, and possibility-based
future = always in motion
emotion = can distort interpretation
prophecy = subject to wording and belief
sensing = may reveal presence or disturbance without exact facts
concealment = possible through distance, discipline, or dark-side clouding
certainty = rare even for powerful practitioners
keysRaw: force vision uncertainty, prophecy uncertainty, future through the force
name: force knowledge limits
keywordsRaw: force vision uncertainty, prophecy uncertainty, future through the force

content:
DEATH AND SURVIVAL
confirmed death = supported by direct event or authoritative record
presumed death = body or proof absent
medical survival = possible through treatment, cybernetics, cloning, or stasis
force ghost = retained identity through selfless force discipline
bound spirit = identity attached to place, object, body, or ritual
return from death = exceptional and mechanism-specific
apparent survival = does not erase prior consequences
keysRaw: presumed dead, survived death, force ghost versus spirit
name: death and survival states
keywordsRaw: presumed dead, survived death, force ghost versus spirit

content:
INFORMATION RELIABILITY
official record = may contain censorship or propaganda
eyewitness = limited by perception and memory
criminal intelligence = transactional and possibly deceptive
ancient archive = incomplete, damaged, or mistranslated
droid record = precise but vulnerable to editing or memory loss
force vision = not documentary evidence
verified fact = supported by multiple compatible sources
keysRaw: rumor versus fact, unreliable record, galactic rumor
name: information reliability
keywordsRaw: rumor versus fact, unreliable record, galactic rumor

content:
IDENTITY LABELS
birth name = personal identity at origin
title = office, rank, honor, or religious status
alias = chosen or assigned alternate identity
callsign = operational identifier
clone designation = production identity that may coexist with chosen name
droid designation = model or individual alphanumeric identity
darth title = sith identity replacing or subordinating prior name
context = determines which label is used
keysRaw: star wars alias, darth title, callsign, designation
name: names titles and aliases
keywordsRaw: star wars alias, darth title, callsign, designation

content:
CONFLICT SCALE
local conflict = settlement or planetary dispute
system conflict = multiple worlds or orbital assets
sector conflict = sustained regional war
galactic war = major powers fight across many sectors
insurgency = decentralized resistance under stronger occupation
criminal war = syndicates contest routes, markets, and territory
scale label = does not imply every world is directly involved
keysRaw: galactic war, sector conflict, planetary conflict
name: galactic conflict scale
keywordsRaw: galactic war, sector conflict, planetary conflict

content:
INSTITUTIONAL CONTINUITY
regime change = rarely replaces every worker or system immediately
bureaucracy = often retained under new leadership
military equipment = reused and rebranded
corporations = adapt contracts to ruling power
local government = may persist under supervision
records = altered, classified, or destroyed
cultural memory = outlives official institutions
keysRaw: institution survives regime change, republic to empire transition, imperial transition
name: institutional continuity
keywordsRaw: institution survives regime change, republic to empire transition, imperial transition
